bad_assumption: Use official CL settlement with an ETF close without checking timestamp
correct_version: CL settlement is around 2:30pm ET; USO and XLE close at 4:00pm ET
why_it_matters: A two-market backtest can create fake intraday alpha from stale futures prices

bad_assumption: Treat the settlement as a clean single closing print
correct_version: The lecture notes the settlement is based on a final-window VWAP around the settlement time
why_it_matters: You need a tradable price that matches the execution clock, not just a convenient data field

bad_assumption: Back-adjusting the futures contract fixes every data problem
correct_version: Back-adjustment removes roll gaps, but it does not fix timestamp mismatch
why_it_matters: Continuous-price hygiene and cross-market execution alignment are separate checks

bad_assumption: Use USO as a clean oil spot hedge
correct_version: USO holds futures exposure; XLE is an equity proxy for oil assets but adds company risk
why_it_matters: USO can cancel the roll edge, while XLE leaves equity and basis risk
